title: azorella prolifera var. hirsuta
intro: Azorella prolifera var. hirsuta, also known as Chupe or Hairy Azorella, is a plant species native to the Andes mountains in South America, belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Azorella prolifera var. hirsuta are attributed to its ability to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach pain, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory conditions. Azorella prolifera var. hirsuta can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various health conditions.
